commit.message:
docs: explain why runtime key length check is unnecessary in proof encoding (#525)

Key length is enforced at insertion time (both direct insert and batch
paths), so keys >=256 bytes can never be stored. The debug_assert!
guards serve as development-time verification only.

Audit finding H2 (false positive).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(ci): improve privacy check — reduce false positives, better output, manual trigger

Context:
The initial privacy check workflow was flagging too many false positives
(comments, placeholders, SVG namespaces, regex patterns, URL parsing)
and triggered unnecessarily on markdown/workflow file changes.

Problem:
- `http://` and `https://` as standalone patterns matched comments,
  placeholders, SVG xmlns, regex literals, and string constants
- `new URL()` matched URL parsing (not network calls)
- Scan ran on `.` catching build artifacts (.svelte-kit/, build/)
- No way to run the workflow manually
- Output didn't clearly show which calls were expected vs unknown

Solution:
- Remove overly broad patterns (http://, https://, new URL) and focus
  on actual network call patterns (fetch, XMLHttpRequest, WebSocket,
  axios/got/node-fetch/ky imports)
- Restrict grep to src/ and src-tauri/src/ instead of repo root
- Add e2e/ and .svelte-kit/ to skip list
- Add workflow_dispatch for manual runs
- Add paths filter so it only triggers on source code changes
- Improve output with clear summary: known calls shown as "ok",
  unknown calls shown as "!!" with blocking exit

Behavior:
- TS scan: finds 0 matches (no actual fetch/XHR calls in source)
- Rust scan: finds 1 known match (reqwest in semantic/model.rs)
- New external calls will fail the build with clear warning

Files:
- .github/workflows/privacy.yml (L1-202): restructured patterns, scoped
  grep paths, added workflow_dispatch, improved output formatting

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: time-aware Night Before section with grouped meal cards

Night Before now adapts to time of day using tod() buckets:
- Morning (5–11am): All day — Breakfast + Lunch + Dinner + Snacks
- Afternoon (11am–6pm): Remaining meals — Lunch + Dinner + Snacks
- Evening (6pm+): Tonight — Dinner only

Carb action bullets are dynamic (spread Xg, ~Yg remaining, ~Zg dinner).
Meal groups render with emoji header + name + subtitle above food cards.
Key numbers always show full-day carb/protein/fat targets.

commit.message:
Full release: trading engine, portfolio intelligence, CLI hardening, and GitHub-ready docs

- Market scanner with 3 strategy signals (momentum, mean-reversion, whale-follow)
- Market regime detection (trending, ranging, volatility, liquidity, whale-dominated)
- Portfolio engine with allocation limits, correlation checks, and rebalancing
- Autopilot trading loop with risk gating, cooldown, and duplicate prevention
- Liquidation risk monitoring and directional exposure controls
- AI command interpreter (Claude + local regex fallback)
- CLI reliability: readline event loop, command history, timeout protection, fast dispatch
- Production hardening: HTTPS enforcement, NaN guards, no hardcoded prices
- Simulation mode defaults to on for safety
- Professional README, LICENSE, .env.example, .gitignore

commit.message:
[Main2Main] Upgrade vLLM to 0303 (#6944)

### What this PR does / why we need it?
break:
- https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/34102 
Disable_full param replaced with valid_modes/invalid_modes API
- https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/35503
Now must return float compilation_time
- https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/35564
New sequence_lengths param added
- https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/33807
A check was performed (if runner_backend != "auto")
- https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/34861
`BaseDeviceCommunicator` now accesses PyTorch's internal `pg_map` to
check process group state
- https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/35274

**Important change:**
- https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/28672

`matcher_utils` directly accesses `torch.ops._C.*` during the import
phase. In the Ascend environment, some unregistered ops trigger
`AttributeError`, causing e2e initialization failure.

https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm-ascend/actions/runs/22607260487/job/65502047131#step:10:2323

https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/blob/main/vllm/compilation/passes/fusion/matcher_utils.py#L29

This PR adds temporary compatibility placeholders (rms_norm,
fused_add_rms_norm, rotate_embedding, static/dynamic fp8 quant,
silu_and_mul) to
`vllm_ascend/patch/platform/patch_fusion_matcher_compat_ops.py` to
ensure no crashes during the import phase. Upstream repairs will be
considered later.

### Does this PR introduce _any_ user-facing change?

### How was this patch tested?

- vLLM version: v0.16.0
- vLLM main:
https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/commit/15d76f74e2fdb12a95ea00f0ca283acf6219a2b7

---------

Signed-off-by: MrZ20 <2609716663@qq.com>
Signed-off-by: gcanlin <canlinguosdu@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: Meihan-chen <jcccx.cmh@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Code <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add server-side auto-assign for Tableau names (rank priority)

On every doGet, processes all unassigned roster members top-down
by rank (owner → manager → jd → l1 → rep). For each, finds
unclaimed Tableau names from their posted DSIs. If exactly one
unclaimed name exists, auto-assigns it and writes to the sheet.
Higher-ranked members claim first so reps get what's left.

commit.message:
feat: mcx mail — interagent message queue with POSIX mail semantics (fixes #132) (#137)

* feat: mcx mail — interagent message queue with POSIX mail semantics (closes #132)

Adds a message queue for structured communication between Claude Code
sessions (worktree agents, managers, human-supervised sessions) using
familiar POSIX mail semantics.

- SQLite `mail` table with sender, recipient, subject, body, threading
- 5 IPC methods: sendMail, readMail, waitForMail, replyToMail, markRead
- CLI `mcx mail` with flags: -s, -H, -u, -r, -N, --wait, --timeout, --for, --from
- Long-poll waitForMail with 30s server-side timeout, client-side retry loop
- Default sender encodes session identity (claude-<cwd-basename> when CLAUDE=1)
- Broadcast support via '*' recipient

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: add IPC server handler tests for mail + defaultSenderName CLAUDE=1 coverage

commit.message:
feat(tun): 实现进程名路由匹配 + UDP 会话并发限制

- 新增 process_windows.go：通过 GetExtendedTcpTable/UdpTable 查询
  连接源端口对应的进程名，传入路由决策（支持 process 规则）
- 新增 process_stub.go：非 Windows 平台存根
- handleTCPConn/handleUDPPacket 现在向 router.Match 传递真实进程名，
  使 config.json 中的 process 规则生效
- 新增 maxUDPSessions=512 限制：同时 UDP 会话超限时丢弃新请求，
  防止游戏流量耗尽系统 socket 缓冲区

用法：在 config.json routing.rules 中加入进程绕过规则，例如：
  {"type":"process","match":"v2rayN.exe","action":"direct"}
  {"type":"process","match":"xray.exe","action":"direct"}

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(deploy): add firewalld trusted zone config for pod CIDRs

On RKE2 with Canal (Calico + Flannel), firewalld's nftables rules at
priority filter+10 run AFTER Calico's iptables rules and REJECT
cross-node pod-to-pod traffic because Calico's cali* veth interfaces
are not in any firewalld zone.

This adds pod (10.42.0.0/16) and service (10.43.0.0/16) CIDRs to
firewalld's trusted zone during bootstrap, fixing the "No route to
host" errors that prevented Istio gateway from routing to upstream
services.

commit.message:
refactor(arrow2): remove parquet2 from daft (#6339)

Remove all parquet2 usage from daft crates, the only remaining usage is
now in arrow2 itself. The arrow-rs reader now handles all parquet
decoding, and the metadata adapter already wraps arrow-rs types, so
parquet2 is fully redundant.

However, we still can't remove `#![allow(deprecated, reason = "arrow2
migration")]` because daft-parquet still uses daft_arrow::array::Array,
daft_arrow::datatypes::Schema, etc. for the pyarrow FFI bridge.

The pyarrow export path (`read_parquet_single_into_arrow`) is rewritten
to call the arrow-rs `read_parquet_single()` and convert the resulting
`RecordBatch` to arrow2 chunks, replacing the parquet2-based
`ParquetReaderBuilder`/`stream_reader` pipeline. For
`StringEncoding::Raw` (reading invalid UTF-8), the parquet metadata is
modified to strip STRING/UTF8 logical types before the arrow-rs reader
sees them, so BYTE_ARRAY columns are decoded as Binary without UTF-8
validation.

---------

* docs: tier-1 rustdocs — World, Entity, reducers, transactions, mutations

Add comprehensive doc comments with cross-links to all major public types:

- World: central store, key methods, concurrency model
- Entity: generational ID packing, recycling, DANGLING sentinel
- ReducerRegistry: registration, dispatch, conflict analysis
- EntityRef/EntityMut: read-only and read-write entity handles
- QueryRef/QueryMut: scheduled query handles
- QueryWriter: buffered transactional iteration with WritableRef
- Spawner: entity creation via lock-free reserve
- DynamicCtx: runtime-validated access handle
- DynamicReducerBuilder: upper-bound access declaration
- Tx: split-phase transaction handle
- Conflict: optimistic validation failure
- SequentialTx: zero-cost direct delegation
- EnumChangeSet: data-driven mutations with rollback
- CommandBuffer: deferred structural changes
- ReducerId/QueryReducerId/DynamicReducerId: typed dispatch handles
